_meta.description: Verified Google search algorithm updates. Used by google_updates_correlator.py to align traffic data with algorithmic events.
_meta.notes: Dates are official Google announcements. 'severity' field is editorial: major = broad observable impact across niches, medium = niche-specific or partial rollout, minor = small adjustment.

updates.name: March 2024 Core Update
updates.notes: 45-day rollout. Helpful Content System integrated into core ranking. Largest core update in recent history.

updates.name: March 2024 Spam Update
updates.notes: Ran concurrently with March 2024 core. New spam policies on expired-domain abuse, scaled content, site-reputation abuse.

updates.name: AI Overviews Launch
updates.notes: AI Overviews (formerly SGE) became generally available in US. Mass CTR compression begins for informational queries.

updates.name: August 2024 Core Update
updates.notes: Aimed to promote high-quality content, demote low-value SEO content.

updates.name: December 2024 Core Update
updates.notes: Unusual back-to-back core update one month after November 2024 core.

updates.name: March 2025 Core Update
updates.notes: Focus on surfacing relevant, high-quality content from all types of sites.

updates.name: August 2025 Spam Update
updates.notes: 27-day rollout. Heavy focus on link spam and site reputation abuse enforcement.

updates.name: December 2025 Core Update
updates.notes: 18-day rollout. Heavy demotion of generic informational content; first-hand experience signals reweighted upward.

updates.name: February 2026 Discover Core Update
updates.notes: Discover-specific ranking update. Began with English US users, expanded to more languages.

updates.name: March 2026 Spam Update
updates.notes: Fastest rollout on record (~1 day). Targeted link manipulation and PBN networks.

updates.name: March 2026 Core Update
updates.notes: 12-day rollout. First-hand experience signals weighted dominant across all niches per E-E-A-T framework.

updates.name: FAQ Rich Result Removal
updates.notes: Google removed FAQ rich result display. Schema FAQPage remains valid for parsing but no longer triggers rich result. Does NOT affect rankings, only SERP appearance.
